name: On gravel from the beach to the Buddhist monastery of Garraf
name_original: En gravel de la playa al monasterio budista del Garraf
description:
This Route proposes a trip from the beaches of Sitges to the popular Buddhist monastery of Garraf, a classic of walking and cycling excursions in the area. After touring the seafront promenade of Sitges, you head inland to visit nearby Sant Pere de Ribes. From there you head towards Olivella and begin the climb to the monastery. The return to Sitges takes you back to the sea, to the Vallcarca cove, and from there to climb again towards the hills of the Garraf massif, a prelude to the final descent to the end of the Route.

The route starts from the Sitges commuter station and goes through the old town through its narrow streets until it takes you directly to its beaches. This part of Sitges has a lot of charm, and includes, in addition to the well-known church of Sant Bartolomeu and Santa Tecla, the rectory, the Maricel palace and the Cau Ferrat museum. After walking the entire seafront of Sitges, you head towards Sant Pere de Ribes, first crossing a section of the old coastal path that leads to Vilanova i la Geltrú, a wide track with very fun slides.

Before reaching Sant Pere de Ribes you pass by the Xoriguera farmhouse, a spectacular modernist palace, an example of the wealth and aesthetic concerns of the area's early 20th century industrialists. Between vineyards and small forests you arrive at Sant Pere de Ribes along the narrow path that leads to its castle, a construction from the 10th century. After crossing the town, you take the Olivella road for three kilometers, to enter again the forest tracks of area. The last stretch of this track narrows until it becomes a fun path, sometimes half-hidden by vegetation.

Then you enter the bottom of the Querol, a track that winds gently ascending into the Garraf park towards the monastery. The climb is momentarily interrupted when descending towards the Masets de Jafra, a couple of hamlets now in ruins. Be careful with the descent, since in some sections the steep slope adds to the loose stone to increase the difficulty. After these ruins you resume the climb towards the monastery. The landscape opens up, and the blue of the sea takes center stage.

After passing through the monastery, a neo-Gothic and modernist palace built by the Indian Pere Domènech i Grau, you begin the descent towards Vallcarca cove, but the path immediately ascends again. Here you find the hardest ramps on the Route, a hard kilometer that takes you to the so-called Sitges track, which returns you to the sea and the end of this route.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Sitges offers diverse landscapes, from the rugged limestone hills of Parc del Garraf Natural Park to scenic coastal paths. The region features a mix of dirt roads and paved sections, providing varied terrain for gravel bikes. Riders can explore deep valleys, rolling hills, and routes that lead through vineyards and natural areas. This varied topography contributes to Sitges' appeal for gravel biking, offering both challenging climbs and scenic trails. Best gravel bike trails around Sitges The most popular gravel bike trail is On gravel from the beach to the Buddhist monastery of Garraf, a 34.5 miles (55.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 17 minutes to complete, traversing varied terrain from the coast into the Garraf Natural Park. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Sakya Tashi Ling Monastery – Sitges Dirt Road loop from Sitges, a difficult 34.1 miles (55.0 km) path. This route offers significant elevation changes and views of the monastery and surrounding natural landscapes. Local gravel bikers also love the Sitges Dirt Road – Vallgrassa Trail loop from Escultura al collectiu homosexual, a 19.4 miles (31.1 km) trail leading through the diverse terrain of the Garraf region, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Gravel biking around Sitges is defined by the rugged limestone landscape of Parc del Garraf, scenic coastal sections, and routes through vineyards. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging excursions with significant elevation gains. The routes in Sitges are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 600 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Sitges's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Sitges?
A: There are over 20 gravel bike routes around Sitges, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 8 moderate routes and 14 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Sitges?
A: Gravel biking in Sitges offers incredibly diverse terrain. You'll encounter a mix of dirt roads and paved sections, often leading through the rugged limestone landscape of the &lt;strong&gt;Parc del Garraf Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes can feature rolling hills, deep valleys, and even sections through vineyards, providing a true Mediterranean contrast.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes in Sitges?
A: Yes, many of the gravel routes around Sitges are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31155827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sakya Tashi Ling Monastery – Sitges Dirt Road loop from Sitges&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that takes you through varied terrain with significant elevation changes. Another great loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitges Dirt Road – Vallgrassa Trail loop from Escultura al collectiu homosexual&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the diverse Garraf region.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the gravel trails?
A: Many gravel routes in Sitges offer stunning views and pass by notable landmarks. You can explore the unique limestone formations of the &lt;strong&gt;Parc del Garraf Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, enjoy coastal vistas, or even cycle past vineyards. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Buddhist Monastery, Sakya Tashi Ling&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle and the Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go gravel biking in Sitges?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Sitges makes it suitable for gravel biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be hot, especially inland, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are mild, making it a good option for escaping colder climates.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel biking options in Sitges?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation and challenging terrain, some sections and shorter routes can be suitable for families with older children or those with good fitness levels. The &lt;strong&gt;Passeig Maritim&lt;/strong&gt; along the coast offers a wide, paved path that can be a gentle starting point or a relaxing finish for a family ride, though it's not a gravel trail itself. For specific gravel routes, look for those described as moderate, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23746495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Ribes – Can Cabanyes farmhouse loop from Sitges&lt;/a&gt;, which is mostly paved.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy the most about gravel biking in Sitges?
A: The gravel biking experience in Sitges is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the rugged beauty of the &lt;strong&gt;Parc del Garraf Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; to the scenic coastal sections and routes through vineyards. The variety of terrain and the challenging climbs are often highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any challenging gravel trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Sitges is known for its challenging gravel routes with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359618679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On gravel from the beach to the Buddhist monastery of Garraf&lt;/a&gt; route covers over 55 km with nearly 900 meters of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31926524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossing over the Foix River – Castellet Castle loop from Sitges&lt;/a&gt;, which spans almost 68 km and includes over 700 meters of climbing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the gravel bike routes?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific regulations for the &lt;strong&gt;Parc del Garraf Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; or any private land you might traverse. Ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry water for them, especially on longer rides or during warmer months.

Q: Are there good viewpoints on the gravel trails around Sitges?
A: Yes, the varied topography of the region, especially within the &lt;strong&gt;Parc del Garraf Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, offers numerous spectacular viewpoints. You'll find panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean coastline, the rugged inland landscapes, and charming villages. Many routes are designed to take advantage of these elevated positions, providing memorable scenic breaks.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for gravel bike routes in Sitges?
A: Gravel bike routes around Sitges vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter rides, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23744129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitges Dirt Road – Vallgrassa Trail loop&lt;/a&gt; at about 31 km, which can be completed in around 2 hours. Longer, more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359618679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On gravel from the beach to the Buddhist monastery of Garraf&lt;/a&gt;, can be over 55 km and take more than 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and fitness.
tips: Sitges is a city famous for its beautiful golden sand beaches, as well as its cultural heritage. Every year, the famous Sitges Film Festival is held here. The town borders the Garraf Park, where you will find green mountains with views of the Mediterranean.

The Xoriguera farmhouse is a modernist palace rebuilt in 1913 from the old medieval farmhouse. The old construction was documented since the 13th century, and it changed owners until at the beginning of the 20th century it was acquired by the Soler family from Vilanova i la Geltrú, who built the current modernist palace on the original farmhouse.

The palace has a basilica plan and three bays, three floors, and a two-slope roof. The tower-viewpoint stands out, crowned with a large pyramidal pinnacle of glazed ceramic. It currently operates as a banquet hall and rural lodging under the name Clos la Plana.

Castillo de Ribes, also called castillo de Bell-lloc or castillo de Sota-ribes, located in the municipality of San Pedro de Ribes (Garraf) declared cultural asset of national interest. Dated in the 10th century and is located at the top of a small steep hill.

The Palau Novella or Garraf Buddhist Monastery is a former Indian house that today is the Sakya Tashi Ling Buddhist monastery. It is located in the Garraf Natural Park, in the Plana Novella urbanization, in the municipality of Olivella.

Of neo-Gothic and modernist style, it was built in the 19th century by Pere Domènech i Grau, an Indian who returned from Cuba after making his fortune there. Years later, the owner, burdened by debt, sold the palace to a community of Augustinian religious, and in 1996 it was acquired by a Buddhist community who restored it and created a museum. The monastery can be visited, more information at https://monjesbudistas.org/

Campdàsens is an aggregate of various buildings, located in the Parc del Garraf, in the municipality of Sitges (Barcelona). Among them is the church of Campdàsens, from the 14th century, a quadrangular defense tower of Romanesque origin and a chapel from the 19th century.

waypoints.name: Garraf Buddhist Monastery, Sakya Tashi Ling
